recent_posts.text: I (actually it was 80% Claude's research) got RCE in a Kubernetes cluster of Kubernetes, but I was one month late. It obviously took weeks for them to reproduce the vulnerability. We are heading to interesting times. It only takes a few minutes to discover vulnerabilities, but weeks to fix them.
Kubernetes: because sometimes you need a hammer for a thumbtack ☸️

Day One is in the books for #KubeConEU! 

Highlight of the day? Our own Michal Nosek speaking about secure databases at scale with our friends over at Chainguard. 

Come by Booth 790 tomorrow to catch some time with our experts!

It also includes the validation steps to actually prove traffic is encrypted.

Balasubramanian Jayamoorthy put together a good walkthrough here. It's worth a read if you're considering deploying #Kubernetes where the internet isn't an option. (4/4)

The guide covers real engineering trade-offs, like why Cilium replaces Flannel and kube-proxy, how WireGuard handles encryption at the kernel level without sidecars, and the version pinning gotchas that can quietly break a cluster. (3/4)

environments means every dependency, image, and binary has to be accounted for before you even start. The article below walks through deploying a full HA K3s cluster with Cilium, #WireGuard encryption, and Rancher, all completely offline. (2/4)

https://lckhd.eu/3LmYA4

There will be situations where you need to run containers in an isolated space. Running Kubernetes in #airGapped 🧵

recent_posts.text: The KubeCon lineup this year reads like a who's who of 'we realized AI needs real infrastructure.' Multi-cluster, observability, networking — all the boring-but-critical stuff that makes AI actually work in production instead of just demos.

Velero going to CNCF sandbox is huge. Backup and DR have been the unsexy but critical gap in the K8s ecosystem. When Broadcom donates something, it usually means they're done investing — but the community might actually make it better.

#KubeConEU Live Coverage, Sri Aradhyula from Cisco shared a practical view of CAIPE: GenAI agents don’t know your intent or desired outcome, people do. The safest path for Greenfield and Brownfield projects alike is clear human-defined goals plus strong end-to-end test coverage....

Software horror: litellm PyPI supply chain attack. Simple `pip install litellm` was enough to exfiltrate SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, Kubernetes configs, git credentials, env vars (all your API keys), shell history, crypto wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD secrets, database passwords.

KubeCon EU happening and the biggest story isn't a new tool — it's NVIDIA donating their GPU DRA driver to Kubernetes.

When the GPU company gives away the scheduling layer, they're not being generous. They're becoming the railroad. You'll buy their trains because the tracks are free.

THIS. This is what I'm telling again and again. The main interest of policy engines is that they're not Kubernetes-only (I mean, beside VAP ofc). But that you can use them to check so many practices.

Option #2 : Kyverno, the option that people love because it's just YAML - essentially VAP but more powerful

And Kyverno allows continuously scanning existing resources, not being admission-only!

Wrapping that first day of  #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon 2026 with a talk by @nabarun.dev comparing the various policy engines options:

- ValidationAdmissionPolicy
- Kyverno
- Gatekeeper/OPA
- Kubewarden
The talk on choosing a policy engine was top-notch, definitely left feeling informed and inspired #kubecon
